publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicles (EVs) has created a significant demand for sustainable and intelligent charging solutions. Traditional charging stations mostly depend on grid power, which increases load demand and indirectly contributes to carbon emissions. To address this challenge, this project proposes an IoT-Enabled EV Parking Station Powered by Renewable Energy. The system utilizes solar photovoltaic panels (or other renewable sources) as the primary energy supply, supported by a battery storage unit to ensure continuous operation. The integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technology makes the station intelligent and user-friendly. IoT modules and sensors are employed to monitor parameters such as energy generation, battery status, charging rate, and slot availability. Real-time data is transmitted to a cloud platform, where users can access it through a mobile application or web interface. This allows EV owners to identify available parking slots, track charging progress, and receive notifications. The proposed system reduces dependency on non-renewable sources, promotes green energy utilization, and enhances user convenience through smart monitoring. Additionally, the project demonstrates scalability for deployment in smart cities, universities, commercial complexes, and residential areas. Thus, this work provides a sustainable and innovative solution to support the growing EV ecosystem.

title: OkraSqueezeNet: A Lightweight CNN with Bayesian Optimization for Real-Time Ladies Finger Leaf Disease Detection
authors: R. Renugadevi, Divyasri M, Rithikaa K, Siby R
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Ladies finger (Abelmoschus esculentus), also name as okra or bhindi, is one important vegetable crop what grow mostly in tropical and subtropical place like India. Farmers is facing big loss many times because of leaf disease cause by fungal, bacterial, and viral thing. This disease not easy to see in starting time, and old way of checking by hand is slow, not correct, and need expert person which many small farmers not have. This work makes OkraSqueezeNet, a light weight brain like network (CNN) for find six common diseases in ladies finger leaf, train with Bayesian setting and can work on phone picture without any man help. A new set of picture name Ladies Finger Leaf Disease (LFLD), having 1,700 farm taken photo in seven group (Good, Alternaria Leaf Spot, Cercospora Leaf Spot, Downy Mildew, Leaf Curly Virus, Phyllosticta Leaf Spot, and Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus), was get from real farm in India. Trick with picture like turn, flip, blur, cut, and light change was use on teach data. The model gets 97.14% right answer with good score in all measure, do better than EfficientNetB0, MobileNetV3, ResNet, InceptionV3, and VGG16. With size only 4.82 MB, it good for put in mobile. An app make with Flutter was also build for farmer to check disease right in field in real time.

title: Using Generative AI to Improve Cities, Energy, Farming, and Healthcare for the Environment
authors: Jitendra Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Generative AI is changing sustainability initiatives in big ways by leveraging advanced machine learning frameworks like GANs, transformer-based models, and diffusion networks. This paper offers an exhaustive examination of generative AI applications across four principal sectors: smart cities, energy systems, agriculture, and healthcare. We show that generative AI applications lead to: (1) a 23–45% improvement in optimizing city traffic, (2) a 15–30% improvement in the accuracy of predicting renewable energy, (3) an 18–35% increase in crop production forecasts, and (4) a 40–60% decrease in the time it takes to find new drugs. This research paper looks at 47 important deployments and presents a unified Generative Sustainability Framework (UGSF) that brings together smart cities, energy, agriculture, and healthcare. It also enables an easy and flexible approach to choose case studies based on certain parameters.

title: LIME-Based Analysis of Adversarial Feature Manipulation in Cloud Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Humberto Goncalves, Zakaria Alomari
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Cloud-based intrusion detection systems face persistent threats from adversarial attacks that introduce imperceptible perturbations to malicious traffic, enabling evasion and compromising network security. While ML-based detection systems demonstrate strong performance under normal conditions, their vulnerability to adversarial manipulation and the mechanisms behind such evasion remain insufficiently understood. This work investigates how adversarial perturbations manipulate feature importance in cloud-based intrusion detection using XGBoost on the CIC-IDS2017 dataset. HopSkipJump attacks were applied to generate adversarial DoS samples, followed by LIME-based interpretability analysis to reveal manipulation patterns. Results show that adversarial attacks strategically suppress DoS-indicative features such as min_seg_size_forward, flow_packets/s, and init_win_bytes_forward by up to 32%, while elevating benign-resembling attributes including destination_port, bwd_packet_length_max, and bwd_header_length. Analysis of misclassified samples reveals non-uniform adversarial strategies that divert model attention from core intrusion indicators through targeted feature weight redistribution. This exploratory study offers actionable insights into vulnerable features and evasion patterns, supporting the development of more robust cloud-based intrusion detection systems that are resilient to adversarial manipulation.

title: Emotionally Adaptive Conversational AI for Personalized Learning
authors: Eman Alduweib, Essa Hezzam, Mohammed Noman
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Computational Intelligence Approaches and Applications (ICCIAA)
abstract: Emotions like confusion, curiosity, and boredom deeply influence learning quality through their impact on attention, motivation, and cognitive regulation. However, virtually all current emotionally adaptive tutoring systems have been developed as reactively designed systems that respond to an individual's emotions once those emotions have occurred, rather than proactively preventing extended frustration or learner disengagement from the learning process. This paper presents a multimodal, uncertainty-aware architecture for Emotionally Adaptive Conversational AI (EACAI), which is designed to move beyond reactively-designed systems and to provide predictive and preventative emotional intelligence in support of enhanced student engagement and learning. The proposed architecture includes four primary components: (1) Multimodal sensing modalities including text-based input, spoken input, behavioral cues, and optionally facial expressions, (2) Confidence weighted multi-modal affective inference models that can accurately infer an individual's emotional state based on the multimodal input data collected during interaction with the EACAI, (3) A Cognitive-Affective Learner Model (CALM-X) that enables longitudinal tracking of an individual's mastery trajectory and emotional states across multiple interactions, and (4) A Predictive Affective Risk Module (PARM) that uses historical data to predict when an individual will be at risk for disengaging, experiencing boredom, or experiencing frustration. Additionally, our architecture integrates several important ethical design elements: consent, transparency, and human oversight. It is also aligned with good educational psychology frameworks, such as Self-Determination Theory and Control-Value Theory, to ensure that the system is both academically sound and ethically acceptable. Finally, evaluation strategies, case studies and ethical considerations are provided.

title: Non-Invasive Diagnosis of Circulating Blood or Cardiac Inflammatory Diseases Through Microwave Technology
authors: Antonio Curcio, Giovanni Lopes, Sandra Costanzo
publish: 2026 IEEE MTT-S International Microwave Biomedical Conference (IMBioC)
abstract: The adoption of microwave technology is proposed in the present work to perform the identification and distinction of different inflammation states associated to cardiac diseases, such as myocarditis, endocarditis, or pericarditis. A well distinguished reflection response is observed when performing non-invasive microwave sensor measurements on plasma samples, thus revealing a correlation between the pathological states and the variation in the dielectric properties of plasma (dielectric constant and conductivity). Experimental results obtained from a measurement campaign conducted on 60 critically ill patients are discussed to validate the proposed approach.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Prompting Strategies for LLM-Based Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Taha Chadli, Asmaa Mourhir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable performance on natural language understanding tasks but struggle with mathematical optimization problems, primarily due to calculation hallucinations and constraint tracking failures. The LLM-based Formalized Programming (LLMFP) framework addresses the above problems by shifting the role of the LLM from a solver to a translator that converts the natural language problems into executable Python code utilizing the Z3 solver. While the framework achieves up to 95% accuracy, its monolithic design introduces limitations such as error propagation, the lack of validation between stages, and parsing difficulties. Error analysis shows that the majority of LLMFP failures are due to formatting and parsing issues rather than reasoning errors. This paper presents the Parser-CoderValidator (PCV) technique, a three-stage modular architecture. The technique decomposes the optimization problem into three stages. First, we establish a new baseline through a comprehensive comparative analysis of the performance of the four state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-OSS-20B, GPT-OSS-120B, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok-Code-Fast-1) on 687 optimization problems across three problem domains: Coffee Supply Chain, Facility Location, and Workforce Scheduling. We then present a comparison of several prompting strategies, demonstrating that PCV achieves up to 39.71% overall accuracy improvements over the LLMFP baseline, with the most dramatic gains in Facility Location. The results underscore the significance of the PCV framework, which demonstrates that careful design of the prompting and validation process is as critical as model selection for achieving reliable LLM-based optimization.

title: BrainArcNet: Attention-Enhanced InceptionResNetV2 with ArcFace for Accurate Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Scans
authors: Snega S, Manikandan J, Shalom Judi Pushpam L
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Brain tumors are among the more fatal neurological problems, where early and accurate diagnosis is a major factor in the success of the treatment. MRI is the most suitable method for tumor detection because of its high-resolution soft-tissue contrast. Still, manual interpretation of MRI scans is a source of inter-observer variability and requires a great deal of expertise. In order to alleviate these issues, we present a deep learning framework BrainArcNet that fuses InceptionResNetV2 as the backbone network merged with a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for the additional feature refinement and an ArcFace classification head to facilitate the angular margin-based separability in the embedding space. The method starts with the carefully planned preprocessing of brain MRIs that include Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), gamma correction, and z-score normalization. These steps help in enhancing contrast and standardizing intensity distributions. The backbone network gets the different-level features from the input images, and CBAM helps the network to selectively focus on the tumor-relevant spatial and channel information. The extracted embeddings are projected into a discriminative hypersphere using ArcFace, leading to improved inter-class margins and intra-class compactness compared to conventional softmax classifiers. Experiments were conducted on the Kaggle Brain Tumor MRI Dataset, which includes 7023 T1-weighted contrast-enhanced images across four types they are glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no tumor. Preliminary results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves ~90– 96% classification accuracy, which is a realistic and achievable performance range for beginner-level implementations when using transfer learning and moderate computational resources. With further finetuning, data augmentation, and hyperparameter optimization, the model has the potential to give more accuracy.

title: Investigating Genomic Insights and Smart Biomaterial–Based Biosensing Using Data Science Approaches for Advancing Precision Oncology Treatments
authors: Sadaf A. Ansari, Irfan Altaf Ansari, Shabina Anjum, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Hrushikesh Madhukar Panchabudhe, Prashant Khobragade
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Cancer is a very heterogeneous disease which is caused by complicated genomic changes that restrict the use of the traditional one-size-fits-all therapy. In precision oncology, the solution to this dilemma is to design treatment plans based on the unique molecular characteristics of each patient; but the large scale, multidimensional, and heterogeneous nature of next-generation sequencing data complicates accurately analyzing and converting this information into clinical practice with only traditional statistical methods. The objective of the study was to explore how integrated methods of data science and smart biomaterial-based bio sensing can support precision oncology, in terms of better biomarker discovery, predicting treatment-response, and providing real-time clinical decision support. It suggested an inclusive model that brings together genomic data preprocessing, statistical variant modelling, machine learning and deep learning based prediction and intelligent biomaterial bio sensing to continuously monitor the tumor. Its methodology included rigorous genomic data cleaning, probabilistic variant discovery, multi-omics integration of features, and predictive modeling with support vector machines, random forests, convolutional neural networks, and transformer architectures, wearable, and implantable biosensors to get longitudinal data on biomarkers. It was shown that deep learning models performed better than classical in genomic-based cancer prediction with an accuracy of up to 96.1 and an F1-score of 95.1. Predictive treatment outcome modeling yielded higher treatment response prediction rates of more than 94 % on breast cancer datasets.

title: A Machine Learning-Assisted Approach for High-Precision PV Module Characterization
authors: Sanat Kumar Patro, Deepak Kumar Barik, Saumyaranjan Sahoo, Gunamani Jena
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Artificial Intelligence &amp; Machine Learning in Communication and Power Systems (AIMLCPS)
abstract: A machine learning-assisted approach for determining the parameters of a dual-diode photovoltaic module model is introduced, utilizing an enhanced differential evolution method with a two-phase mutation technique, referred to as Multimodal Multi-Objective Differential Evolution with Two-Stage Mutation and Modified Maximum Extension Distance (MMODE-TSM-MMED). This strategy is designed to enhance diversity and accelerate convergence. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is assessed by comparing the derived outcomes with those of alternative algorithms. Observations indicate that the optimal solution stabilizes at 6.682 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> across all iterations, with a maximum iteration count of fewer than 15.

title: CityPulse 360 – AI-Powered Real-Time Urban Happiness and Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sai Charan Naidu P, J Naveen Krishna, A Akash, J Vijayarani
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: The rapidly growing urbanization in India has introduced a demand of intelligent tools that will be used to monitor the well-being of the city in real time. Traditional urban performance indicators utilize stagnant or lagging data and can be unable to reflect the present public feeling. CityPulse 360 brings in an AI-based system merging social media mood, environmental factors, price-of-living information, and infrastructure indicators and produce a real-time mood of happiness in Indian cities. The system uses Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Machine Learning (ML) to process live streams in the format of weather and air-quality data provided by OpenWeatherMap API, sentiment-based data provided by Twitter, Reddit, and YouTube, and structured data in the form of safety, healthcare, affordability. These multimodal inputs are aggregated into composite scores and represented with the help of interactive Power BI dashboard. In this paper, the architecture of the system, data acquisition, preprocessing, feature engineering, and dashboard design will be presented to monitor the urban happiness. The framework provides assistance to policy makers, researchers and citizens by providing an ever-present and multi-dimensional picture of urban well-being.

title: Deepfake Video Forensics Using ResNeXt–LSTM Visual Modeling and DSP-Enhanced Audio Spectrogram Analysis
authors: Tanish Lal O, Sijo Devassy V J, P. Deepa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: The technology for creating deepfakes has advanced rapidly, and this allows for realistic modifications to both audio and video. Many current forensic systems only use frame-based visual artefacts, while modern deepfake pipelines use temporal smoothing, high-quality generative models, and synthetic voice, which makes unimodal detectors less dependable. This research introduces a multimodal deepfake detection framework that integrates a ResNeXt50 video feature extractor, an Long Short Term Memory (LSTM)-based temporal analysis module, and a Digital Signal Processing (DSP)-enhanced audio spectrogram classifier. Visual characteristics find differences in faces, temporal modelling finds differences between frames, and the audio stream finds problems in synthetic speech, like odd harmonic patterns and smoothed spectrum energy. A fusion module takes predictions from all branches and merges them to give a single authenticity score. Experiments show that using both DSP-based audio cues and temporal modelling makes things far more stable than only using video. The system is set up using a Django web interface that is built for automatic real-time deepfake verification.

title: A Dual-Level Data-Driven Framework for Reducing Financial Fraud in Banking
authors: Nusrat Jahan, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Anika Sayeema, Sabiha Firdaus, Jannatul Maua, Hashibul Ahsan Shoaib
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Pervasive Computing and Social Networking (ICPCSN)
abstract: Financial fraud detection remains a major challenge in modern banking due to evolving adversarial manipulation, data imbalance, and the narrow focus of conventional detection strategies. This paper introduces a dual-level data-driven framework that combines account-level behavioral risk modeling with transaction-level anomaly detection to enable more accurate and context-aware fraud identification. The proposed system achieves an ROC-AUC of 0.995 and AUPRC of 0.907 under the final combined inference model, surpassing traditional classifiers such as Logistic Regression (AUPRC 0.716) and even deep MLP models (AUPRC 0.843). Under stringent operating requirements of 90% precision, the proposed model maintains a recall of 0.611 compared to 0.519 for deep MLP and 0.381 for Logistic Regression. Additionally, the fairness-aware account model reduces demographic prediction disparity from 0.178 to 0.081. These results, validated on both the Credit Card Fraud dataset and the BAF account dataset, demonstrate that multi-scale risk reasoning substantially improves operational fraud detection performance and provides practical benefit for real-world banking applications and fraud monitoring workflows.

title: Performance Comparison of Radiative Transfer Simulation Tools Based on Land Surface Radiance in Beihang Green Garden
authors: Yunda Yang, Huaiwen Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: To evaluate the performance of radiation transfer simulation tools in ground object radiance modeling, this study conducted research at Beijing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics' Lvyuan campus, selecting typical ground objects including vegetation, water bodies, soil, and buildings. Radiance data were extracted from Gaofen-1 satellite imagery and ASTGM TM V003 elevation data, with simulation scenarios constructed using three projection methods: fisheye camera, parallel projection camera, and perspective camera. Three mainstream radiation transfer simulation tools—LESS, DART, and ERADIATE—were employed for radiance modeling. A quantitative evaluation system was established, including absolute radiance error, relative error, root mean square error (RMSE), and coefficient of determination, combined with qualitative dimensions such as morphological fidelity and scene adaptability to systematically compare simulation accuracy under various solar geometric conditions. Results demonstrated that the LESS model consistently outperformed others across all observation conditions, maintaining a stable coefficient of determination above 0.99 and achieving the lowest RMSE across all experimental groups, showcasing superior numerical stability and morphological fidelity. Leveraging ray tracing algorithms, LESS demonstrated significant advantages in complex 3D scene simulations (vegetation-water-body-building mixtures), particularly excelling in fine-grained vegetation canopy structure representation, making it an ideal tool for scientific quantitative remote sensing inversion. The DART model exhibited balanced performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> generally above 0.95 and RMSE slightly higher than LESS but still within acceptable ranges. DART demonstrates robust performance in multiscale simulations (from individual blades to landscape scales) and multi-angle radiative transfer analysis, particularly exhibiting strong adaptability when handling heterogeneous surfaces, making it suitable for engineering simulation validation and parameter inversion applications. The ERADIATE model shows significant uncertainty in complex projection conditions, with RMSE values notably higher than the other two models. While its high-precision radiative transfer algorithm performs well in idealized simple scenarios, simulation accuracy and stability are relatively limited in highly heterogeneous urban environments like Beihang Green Park due to constraints in scene modeling flexibility and computational resource availability. However, ERADIATE demonstrates lower coefficient of variation under weak signal conditions (e.g., cloudy days or water bodies), indicating its advantages under low-energy input scenarios. This study provides quantitative evidence for selecting and optimizing radiation transfer simulation tools in complex surface environments, while establishing methodological foundations for precision evaluation and parameter calibration of simulation tools in quantitative remote sensing applications.

title: Dynamic Data-Driven Framework for Forecasting Employment Trends in the AI Era
authors: Mayank Singh Negi, Vansh Raheja, Ketan Kumar, Meena Chaudhary, Gunjan Chandwani, Narender Gautam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Automation for Sustainable Solutions (ICASS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping global labor markets by automating repetitive tasks while creating new opportunities in data-driven and technology-intensive sectors. This paper presents a Dynamic Data-Driven Employment Forecasting Model (DDEFM) that predicts employment trends using a hybrid machine learning framework integrating time-series forecasting, natural language processing, and sectoral analysis. The model combines real-world labor datasets, AI adoption metrics, and investment indicators to evaluate employment volatility across sectors. The research contributes a technical methodology to forecast job creation and decline across industries such as healthcare, IT, education, and green energy. Experimental results demonstrate the model’s ability to reduce prediction error by 12–18% compared to baseline statistical models. This study provides a scalable framework for policymakers and industries to plan workforce development in the AI era.

title: Harnessing Large Language Models for Next-Generation Artificial Intelligence Applications
authors: Navin Garg, Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Tharakesavulu Vangalapat, Mina Kumari, Mahesh Basavaraj, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) usage is the innovation capable of introducing the new generation of the artificial intelligence system with adaptive thinking, multimodal understanding, and grounded knowledge synthesis. Transformer architecture can be developed; less supervised scalable cognitive automation can be enabled by combining developed augmented retrieval-based generation and parameter-efficient tuning. This paper presents a technically valid and conceptually coherent hybrid-grounded LLM model that has cross-modal embedding alignment, external-knowledge recall, symbolical equivalent validation, and, the hallucination-controlled decoding. The methodology guarantees the semantic integrity and logic-sensitive decision-support in the different fields. The experimental evaluation of a group of complex reasoning demonstrates a considerable improvement as the number of perplexities was decreased to 7.1 instead of 16.8, the level of overall accuracy was increased by 63.4 to 83.8 percent, and the level of F1-score improved by 65.1 to 85.4. Generative metrics also show a significant improvement as BLEU scores 36.9, ROUGE-L scores 56.4 and exact match scores also goes to 63.2 and expectation calibration error also goes down to 3.6 and hallucination error also drops to 5.1. These findings indicate that grounded LLM design is an experimental catalyst of the future AI ecosystems with central interests on reliability, knowledge validity and high-fidelity reason.

title: An AI-Driven Optimization Framework for Sustainable E-Waste Management and Resource Utilization
authors: Priyanka Chauhan, Deepak Kumar Adhana, Mayank Saxena
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Big Data &amp; Machine Learning (ICBDML)
abstract: In the given paper, an AI-based optimization model of sustainable e-waste management and resource use is presented. The research falls in the fields of circular economy engineering, intelligent decision analytics, and intelligent urban sustainability systems. The framework creates a spatio-temporal digital twin of e-waste flows, which incorporates graph neural prediction of waste generation, deep reinforcement learning prediction of adaptive collection and routing, and multi-objective evolutionary optimization prediction of processing and material recovery. Lifecycle sustainability assessment is incorporated to pursue, all at the same time, cost, environmental impact, and circularity goals. Experimental assessment on the city scale shows that there are very high increments in performances relative to traditional methods that depend on rules to use in collection, logistics systems relying on optimization and, hybrid AI logistics systems. The efficiency of the proposed framework is 93% material recovery efficiency, 23.1% reduction on greenhouse gases, 25.6% operational costs efficiency and 27.4% reduction on waste which is not controlled. Further measurements ensure better routing effectiveness, increased service completion, greater prediction performance, increased processing throughput, greater circularity gain, and greater stability of policy compliance. These findings prove the potential of the framework to convert e-waste ecosystems into smart, resilient, and sustainability-oriented resource infrastructures of the contemporary smart cities.

title: Research on Construction and Virtual Commissioning of Intelligent Warehousing System Based on PQFactory Platform
authors: Runsheng Hao, Guohao Feng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Sensors and Information Technology (ICSI)
abstract: Digital twin virtual simulation technology plays a significant role in the virtual commissioning of intelligent production lines. Based on the PQFactory platform, this paper conducts virtual commissioning research on the intelligent warehousing system design. A digital model of the warehousing system is designed, a digital twin scenario is built on the PQFactory platform, and relevant parameters are configured according to the process flow. The robot program and PLC control program are compiled, and the KingIOServer gateway is used to realize communication between the physical PLC and the PQFactory platform, completing the integrated digital twin commissioning of the entire system. The virtual simulation verifies the accuracy and reliability of the intelligent warehousing system design, which helps improve the design and commissioning efficiency and reduce the development cycle and cost.

title: New approach to improve low-noise amplifier performances for 2.45 GHz wireless applications
authors: Moustapha Séré, Jamal Zbitou
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This article describes a new approach to improving the performance of low-noise amplifiers (LNA) operating at 2.45 GHz. It focuses on the use of novel circuit topologies, advanced techniques to reduce the noise figure (NF), and simultaneous gain enhancement. Such improvement is made possible by advancements in CMOS technologies, such as gallium arsenide (GaAs), and the careful selection of impedance matching network components. The GaAs-based ATF-21170 transistor is used as the active element in the proposed LNA. This LNA operates in class $\mathbf{A B}$ and is designed using the Advanced Design System (ADS) software. Simulations yield a gain of 15.918 dB and a noise figure of $\mathbf{1. 1 4 3 ~ d B}$. The input (S11) and output (S22) reflection coefficients are -20.136 dB and -15.205 dB, respectively. The implementation layout measures $\mathbf{4 0} \times \mathbf{8 3. 5} \mathbf{~ m m}^{\mathbf{2}}$. The final results demonstrate enhanced performance for receivers used in wireless communications, such as radar systems, Wi-Fi, and satellite communications…

title: FDSCBAM-YOLO: Integrating Frequency-Domain Attention with Depthwise Separable Convolution for Data-Scarce SAR Vehicle Detection
authors: Dan Gao, Shuo Yu, Caoyi Mei, Xiaofang Wu, Zhijin Wen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Geology, Mapping and Remote Sensing (ICGMRS)
abstract: The intelligent advancement of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Automatic Target Recognition (ATR) is frequently hindered by the scarcity of high-quality annotated data. To address this fundamental challenge, we propose FDSCBAM-YOLO, a lightweight detection network incorporating a novel Frequency-domain Depthwise Separable Convolutional Attention Module (FDSCBAM). By synergistically integrating depthwise separable convolutions with both frequency-domain and spatial attention mechanisms, FDSCBAM effectively suppresses inherent coherent speckle noise and accentuates target scattering features in the frequency domain without compromising computational efficiency. Furthermore, we introduce a frequency-domain enhanced loss function specifically tailored to improve small target detection. Extensive experiments conducted under data-scarce conditions demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior detection accuracy while maintaining low model complexity.

title: PSO-Based Optimization of a CMOS Switched-Capacitance Current-Starved Ring VCO in 0.18-μm Technology
authors: Elhafidi Sanae, Aymen Hal-Houssine, Maaroufi Ismail, El Beqal Asmae, Benhala Bachir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a switched-capacitance current-starved ring voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO). The proposed architecture relies on a current-starved topology combined with switched MOS capacitance tuning. The circuit is implemented in TSMC 0.18 μm CMOS technology. It operates at a center oscillation frequency of 1.5 GHz under a 1.8 V supply voltage. The main performance metrics considered are phase noise and power consumption. Design trade-offs inherent to multi-stage ring VCOs are thoroughly investigated. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is adopted and applied as a metaheuristic optimization strategy to identify optimal transistor sizing and tuning parameters. The optimization process targets low phase noise and reduced power dissipation. The complete VCO is designed and simulated using the Cadence design environment. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed methodology. The optimized VCO achieves a phase noise of –80 dBc/Hz at a 1 MHz offset. The total power consumption is limited to 15.8 mW. These results demonstrate the suitability of PSO for highperformance CMOS Current-Starved ring VCO design.

title: Dual-Path Motion Encoding for Robust Violence Detection
authors: Abderrahim Ouza, Mohamed El Ghmary, Ali Choukri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: In this paper we tackle the problem of violent event detection in realistic crowd scenes, which combine small scale individual actions with large scale group movement. We propose a dual-path motion encoding framework that jointly models fine-grained human micro-motions and global crowd dynamics. The local branch captures spatiotemporal human-centric features using 3D convolutions, while the global branch models scene-level temporal evolution through a bidirectional recurrent encoder. A stable training strategy combining warm-up scheduling and adaptive learning rate reduction ensures reliable convergence. Experimental results on the Real-Life Violence Situations dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms CNN, LSTM, and 3D CNN baselines in terms of accuracy and F1score. Visualization analysis confirms that the two branches learn complementary motion patterns. The method is suitable for realtime surveillance applications.

title: IAC-TecMed Database for Infrared Thermography-Based Diabetic Foot Monitoring
authors: Badiaa Ait Ahmed, Pilar Montañés-Rodríguez, Otman Aghzout, Juan Ruiz-Alzola
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Innovative Research in Applied Science, Engineering and Technology (IRASET)
abstract: This publication presents a publicly available subset of a database created to assess diabetic foot anomalies using infrared thermography, aimed at enhancing early detection of complications in diabetic patients. The initial study involved 22 healthy subjects (9 females and 13 males) to develop a standard morphological model for evaluating temperature asymmetry in the plantar aspects of both feet. The database comprises thermographic and RGB-D images captured at four time points (T0, T5, T10, T15) under controlled conditions (temperature: $\mathbf{2 5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{C}$, constant luminosity, and humidity), resulting in a longitudinal dataset that reflects natural thermal variations. This methodology is clinically relevant as it identifies thermal anomalies that may prevent complications in diabetic foot. Images were subjected to segmentation masks, manually delineated by three experienced researchers, and validated via the STAPLE algorithm to establish a ground truth. Statistical analyses utilized Wilcoxon’s matched-pairs test, with adjustments for multiple comparisons via Hochberg and false discovery rate methods, maintaining a significance level of 5%. These analyses revealed significant temperature variation areas, reinforcing the validity of the results. From the cohort, 19 participants (7 females and 12 males) consented to share their data for research. This subdataset (IAC-TecMed database, hereafter IAC-TMBD) is a valuable resource for exploring thermal patterns and their implications in foot disorder monitoring. This subset retains the integrity and scope of the original data, providing a resource for further investigation into temperature patterns and their implications for early detection and monitoring of foot disorders.

title: Koopman Neural Operator for Multi-Task Modeling in Optical Fiber Communications with NTK Analysis
authors: Yiming Meng, Xiao Luo, Xiaotian Jiang, Wenbin Chen, Shengnan Li, Min Zhang, Danshi Wang
abstract: Optical fiber communication networks serve as a critical backbone of modern information infrastructure and have undergone rapid development in recent decades. To optimize transmission dimension resources, extensive research has focused on accurately modeling key linear and nonlinear effects, including chromatic dispersion, Kerr nonlinearity, stimulated Raman scattering, laser transition processes, and light field evolution, all governed by partial differential equations (PDEs). Traditional numerical solvers and black-box data-driven neural networks are commonly used to solve these PDEs but suffer from high computational complexity and strong data dependency, respectively. This paper explores the Koopman neural operator (KNO) for multi-task modeling in optical communications. The framework is validated across four representative tasks: 16QAM waveform propagation, quality of transmission (QoT) estimation, optical amplification modeling, and light field evolution. In all benchmarks, KNO outperforms Fourier neural operators (FNO) and convolutional neural operators (CNO), reducing mean squared error by up to one order of magnitude in certain tasks, requiring approximately three times fewer parameters. Branch ablation studies on spectral components reveal that KNO's dual-branch architecture adaptively balances low-frequency (LF) global dynamics and high-frequency (HF) local details. Furthermore, generalization tests under unseen physical conditions, such as launch powers and cross-device generalization, demonstrate KNO's robustness in handling conditions not observed during training. Neural tangent kernel (NTK) analysis highlights KNO's superior spectral properties, such as higher effective rank, lower condition number, and improved kernel ridge regression performance, which underpin its enhanced trainability, modal response, and generalization. This work establishes KNO as a scalable surrogate for PDE-related problems in optical fiber communication systems.

title: Geometry Optimization of Non-Ideal Through-Glass Vias (TGVs) for Enhanced Electrical and Mechanical Performance
authors: Zhen Fang, Jinxu Liu, Borui Li, Wenlei Li, Jihua Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Through-glass vias (TGVs) have attracted considerable attention in advanced packaging because they offer low dielectric loss and good electrical insulation. However, the tapered sidewalls formed during fabrication can affect the reliability of glass-based packages. These sidewalls increase the parasitic inductance at high frequencies and cause nonuniform stress under thermal loading. Existing TGV inductance models still have limited ability to describe skin-current effects in nonvertical vias. This study presents an analytical model for tapered TGVs that includes skin current and defines the frequency range in which the model is applicable. The effect of taper angle on stress distribution at 200 °C is also examined. The results show that the taper angle redistributes stress in the copper column and reduces stress concentration at the Cu/glass interface. By further optimizing laser power, ultrasonic power, HF concentration, etching temperature, and ammonium fluoride concentration, precise control of the TGV taper is achieved. This work provides a design reference and an optimization strategy for high-performance glass interposers.

title: Parameter Efficient Emotional Transfer for Fine-Grained Emotional Audio-Driven Talking Portrait Generation_supp1-3699022.mp4
authors: Chi-Man Pun
abstract: Facial expression, especially emotion, plays a crucial role in building a realistic talking portrait video from audio. Previous emotional talking head methods train lip-synchronization with video-level annotated emotion labels jointly. However, the video-level annotated emotion labels make it hard to learn the accurate frame-wise emotions. Besides, since the emotional dataset is much smaller than the neutral faces, it becomes harder to generate lip-synchronized portrait videos with accurate expressions. To address these challenges, we propose a new method for efficient emotional transfer and detailed emotion capture. In detail, we first consider emotions as the residual of the neutral expression and train additional adapters (LoRAs in our case) for each emotion individually. Then, we use the frame-wise emotional annotations as input and design a novel method to jointly optimize the multiple adapters. After training, our method can produce high visual quality results by driving audio and fine-grained emotion guidance with negligible additional parameters. Extensive experiments also demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in synthesizing photo-realistic, emotionally expressive talking portraits that are well-synchronized with the input audio compared with other state-of-the-art methods.

title: The Capacity of the Weighted Read Channel
authors: Omer Yerushalmi, Tuvi Etzion, Eitan Yaakobi
abstract: Nanopore sequencing is emerging as a powerful tool for storing digital information in DNA molecules. Its advantages over traditional sequencing methods such as support for long reads, scalability, and relatively low cost, making it an attractive and growing area of research. A fundamental aspect of this technology is the nature of its output: instead of reading individual bases in isolation, the sequencer observes overlapping segments of the DNA sequence due to inter-symbol interference (ISI). Similar readout mechanisms also arise in emerging memory technologies such as domain wall (racetrack) memory, where transverse-read techniques can sense the number of stored bits within a region without shifting any domains. Motivated by this analogy, we study theoretical models that capture the fundamental structure of such overlapping read processes. In this work, we study a simplified model of the nanopore sequencing process, known as the (ℓ, δ)-read channel. This channel operates by applying a sliding window of length ℓ across a DNA sequence and shifting it by δ positions after each read. The output of each window is a numerical value, which, in our model, corresponds to the sum (or Hamming weight) of the bases within that window. The collection of these outputs forms what we refer to as the read vector. Due to overlapping windows, multiple input sequences can map to the same read vector, leading to ambiguity. The main object of interest in this work is the capacity of the (ℓ, δ)-read channel, defined as the number of distinct output sequences that can be generate by this channel. Previous studies have determined the capacity for specific combinations of ℓ and δ, such as when ℓ ≤ δ, when ℓ is a multiple of δ, and for certain small values of ℓ and δ. In this study, we extend the current knowledge by addressing more general cases. Our first main result is a closed-form expression for the capacity when δ < ℓ < 2δ, given by: 1/δ log2 ( 1/2 (ℓ + 1 + √(ℓ + 1)2 − 4(ℓ − δ)(ℓ − δ + 1) )). This result is derived by analyzing a deterministic finite-state representation of the read channel and applying tools from graph theory, particularly the Perron–Frobenius Theorem. Next, we consider the case where ℓ ≥ 2δ and δ does not divide ℓ. For this setting, we construct a constrained code that yields an upper bound on the capacity, improving upon known bounds and further refining our understanding of this more complex regime. Finally, we extend the model to the two-dimensional setting. Motivated by the physical layout of some sequencing platforms and potential future applications, we propose and analyze a two-dimensional version of the weighted read channel and we present initial results on its capacity.

title: Finite-Time LQR Control With Redundant Inputs for Continuous-Time Systems
authors: Jianzhou Liu, Ze Zhang, Hongzheng Quan, Tianliang Zhang
abstract: This article investigates the effects of redundant control inputs on the finite-time linear quadratic regulator (LQR). Despite the widespread application of finite-time LQR control, characterizing the impact of redundancy remains challenging due to its time-varying nature. To address this gap, we first derive novel lower and upper bounds for the solution of the Riccati matrix differential equation by leveraging key matrix and eigenvalue inequalities. Building on these bounds, we establish a sufficient condition that guarantees a simultaneous reduction in both the optimal quadratic performance index and the controller gain. Furthermore, a deterministic algorithm is presented to facilitate the practical design of the weighting matrices. The effectiveness and engineering applicability of the proposed approach are demonstrated through numerical simulations and a case study on a fluid catalytic cracking reactor.

title: Target-centered Micro-motion Tracking and Measurement with Commodity WiFi
authors: Zijun Han, Zixuan Huang, Shurong Ge, Zhaoming Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wireless signals-based radio tracking and measurement has emerged as a significant avenue for innovation in human-computer interaction. Among various wireless sensing modalities such as acoustic signals, visible light, Radio Frequency (RF), etc., the WiFi-based technique holds significant promise owing to its pervasiveness and cost-effectiveness. However, constrained by the limitations ofWiFi’s low frequency, small antenna array and bandwidth, current state-of-the-art systems can only achieve an accuracy on the order of decimeters. Many sophisticated micro-motion applications, such as aerial handwriting tracking, necessitate a significantly higher level of accuracy than what current state-of-the-arts can provide. In this paper, we present MicroFi, which targets to measure the micro-motion of WiFi radios in view of the Channel State Information (CSI). To be specific, we devise an innovative inside-out incremental motion tracking model to convert the tracking task from a transceiver view to a target-centered view. This model operates independently of any prior knowledge regarding anchor locations and demonstrates robustness against occlusions. Additionally, we propose a two-way communication protocol for CSI bidirectional collection, and eliminate clock asynchronization-induced random frequency offsets by channel reciprocity. We employ a 3D Multiple Signal Classification algorithm to jointly estimate the arrival angle, time of flight and Doppler velocity of the tracking target, and then derive the motion traces by multi-anchor aggregation. We have prototyped MicroFi utilizing commercially available WiFi radios, and comprehensive experiments confirm that MicroFi is capable of achieving an accuracy of 1.8 cm across a variety of user scenarios and environmental conditions, positioning it as a promising solution for numerous motion measurement applications.

title: Rate Optimization in Hybrid Multi-RIS Assisted NOMA Systems Based on WMMSE Algorithm
authors: Zhongrui Shu, Hong Wang, Youhong Feng, Peiyu Wang, Heyi Li
abstract: Both reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) and non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) are emerging technologies poised to revolutionize future wireless communications. The active RIS plays a crucial role in mitigating the severe path attenuation of multi-hop reflected links. This paper addresses the sum rate optimization problem in a hybrid cascaded RIS system, considering the constraints on power budget and phase shift of each reflection element. In order to tackle the formu lated nonconvex problem, a weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) algorithm is invoked to optimize the receiver, beamforming vector, and active load amplitude. Furthermore, the phase shifts of each RIS are calculated by using a phase rotation algorithm, followed by multiple rounds of alternating optimization. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme achieves the optimal data rate in various communication environments and outperforms the existing approaches significantly.

title: Systematic Evaluation of Interpolation Strategies for Soil Moisture Retrieval Using Sentinel-1/2 Data: A Case Study in Shandian River Basin
authors: Dongqiang Yang, Changhua Liu, Haobo Cai
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Microwave remote sensing is widely used for soil moisture (SM) monitoring due to its sensitivity to dielectric properties. However, microwave backscattering is affected by surface roughness, vegetation structure, and soil texture, which limits high-resolution SM retrieval accuracy and necessitates multi source data integration. Optical remote sensing provides complementary vegetation information but suffers from data gaps caused by cloud cover. Although various gap-filling methods for vegetation indices have been compared, their impacts on multi source SM retrieval remain underexplored. This study integrates Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data through random forest (RF) regression in northern China's Shandian River Basin, comparing univariate and multivariate interpolation methods for SM retrieval. Comprehensive evaluation includes spatial cross validation, leave-one-station-out (LOSO) validation, Station Based Spatial Error Assessment and feature importance assessment. Results demonstrate that interpolation methods significantly improve SM retrieval accuracy. The Savitzky-Golay (SG) filter achieves highest accuracy (R = 0.849), while Multivariate Singular Spectrum Analysis (MSSA) demonstrates superior spatial generalization. This study provides quantitative guidance for interpolation method selection in agro-pastoral transitional zones.

title: Finite-Time Distributed Time-Varying Optimization of High-Order Agents With Unknown Gradient Derivative
authors: Wenbo Zhu, Shu Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: In this paper, the finite-time distributed time-varying optimization problems for high-order agents are investigated, where the gradient derivative of time-varying cost functions is unknown. First, a finite-time distributed optimal trajectory estimator which do not rely on the Hessian information and the partial derivative of gradient with respect to time is constructed. Then, based on the generated optimal trajectory estimation signal, a distributed time-varying optimization algorithm for high-order agents with globally practically finite-time convergence is designed, where the bound of optimization error can be arbitrarily small by regulating controller parameters. Moreover, the rigorous convergence analysis is provided as well. In final, a simulation example is conducted to validate the proposed theoretical results.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp2-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. A simplified mechanics-based anchoring model captures the dominant directional friction asymmetry induced by foot geometry and guides parameter optimization. Experiments demonstrate a maximum friction anisotropy ratio of 4.75, enabling stable crawling at 0.40 mm/s horizontally and 0.13 mm/s vertically in 4–9 mm pipelines, including complex three dimensional curved configurations. Blind inspection experiments with an onboard micro-camera further validate the robot's potential for inspection in highly confined pipeline environments.

title: A Soft Worm Robot for Sub-Centimeter Scale Pipeline Inspection_supp4-3699247.mp4
authors: Yitong Zhou
abstract: Inspection of sub-centimeter pipelines with continuous spatial curvatures remains challenging due to extreme geo metric confinement. This paper presents an ultralight, trunkless worm-inspired robot actuated by a single twisted and coiled actuator (TCA) specifically optimized for short-range inspection within localized extreme spatial bottlenecks. By integrating actuation and structural support into a single TCA and eliminating rigid trunks and auxiliary recovery mechanisms, the robot achieves a compact and lightweight design, with a mass of 0.5–1.1 g, a length of 32 mm, and a diameter of 4–10 mm. Directional peristaltic locomotion is enabled by a wedge-based micro silicone frustum foot that provides passive frictional anisotropy without complex control. title: Graph Processing with Contrastive Learning for Social Recommendation in IoT Interaction Networks
authors: Yan Wang, Yang Ma, Aijia Ouyang, Junyi Zhang, Weilong Peng
abstract: AI agents deployed in Internet of Things (IoT) environments increasingly rely on interaction data to perform adaptive decision-making and personalized service delivery. Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based recommendation models have demonstrated strong capabilities in modeling user preferences by leveraging relational information. However, IoT interaction networks often contain noisy or preference-irrelevant social connections that violate the homophily assumption, which may negatively affect information propagation and decision accuracy. Moreover, observed interaction data are typically sparse and may omit valuable implicit feedback, especially in dynamic IoT environments. To address these challenges, we propose GPCL, a graph processing framework with contrastive learning for adaptive AI agents in IoT interaction networks. GPCL progressively denoises the social graph by adaptively pruning low-confidence user–user links, while enriching the interaction graph by introducing high-confidence yet unobserved interactions. By jointly refining both relational structures, the framework improves structural quality and enhances representation reliability for downstream decision tasks. In addition, GPCL incorporates a lightweight embedding-level contrastive learning strategy that enhances representation robustness without relying on costly graph perturbations, enabling efficient representation learning. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets—Yelp, Ciao, and LastFM—demonstrate that GPCL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides an effective and efficient solution for adaptive decision modules of AI agents in IoT interaction networks, particularly under sparse and noisy relational environments.

title: A Heterogeneous System Architecture for Monocular Depth Estimation and Parallel Multi-Task Vision on Edge Devices
authors: Donghua Chen, Runtong Zhang, Dandan Li
abstract: To enhance the accuracy and resource utilization of monocular camera-based edge artificial intelligence (AI) applications, we introduce a heterogeneous system architecture for parallel multi-task vision tailored for resource-constrained edge devices. We developed an experimental self-powered vision system integrating a cutting-edge AI-enabled IMX500 vision sensor and a Hailo-8 AI accelerator. This architecture enables ultra-low power sensing and edge inferencing, successfully addressing the on-device challenges of multi-task sensor fusion in object detection, pose analysis, distance estimation, and post-processing decision support. The framework features a multi-objective and multi-stage intelligent visual perception architecture utilizing pretrained light-weight models. By incorporating state-of-the-art techniques such as YOLO, FastDepth, Depth Prediction Transformers and Lite-Mono, we achieve robust parallel sensing and inferencing within the constraints of monocular vision. Through comprehensive testing and evaluation on multiple datasets, our results demonstrate that the proposed architecture enhances complex parallel vision tasks while maintaining both high performance and a small form factor. Our work provides sustainable and scalable hardware-software co-design solutions for intelligent applications, including autonomous vehicles, smart robots, mobile assistants, and interactive devices.

title: Detection of Arbitrary Polarization States Based on Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Yi Ren, Jun Xia, Yuxiang Wang, Dianjie Wang, Jingjing Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Polarization detection is crucial for advanced electromagnetic applications, yet conventional Stokes-based methods are often bulky and complex. This letter proposes a universal, compact approach for detecting arbitrary polarization states on the Poincaré sphere using a spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) platform. Leveraging the spin-momentum locking property of SSPPs, the design incorporates only two spiral shaped scatterers coupled to a single SSPP waveguide. Incident polarization is decomposed into left- and right-handed circular components, which are separated directionally and collected at two output ports. The signal ratio between these ports yields the full polarization state via a derived analytical relation, eliminating the need for multi-element cascades or Stokes parameter retrieval. Simulated and measured deviations are below 0.9 ° and 8.5 °, respectively. The proposed platform offers a fabrication-friendly, planar architecture suited for on-chip integration, providing a robust miniaturized solution for polarization sensing, communication, and imaging.
